meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No Project Ideas (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Struggling with no project ideas? Learn how to use Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build your first professional-grade portfolio project fast.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. Use AI to scaffold your project foundation while Git manages the versioning complexity. By pairing Antigravity’s architectural generation with Claude Code’s CLI execution, you transform a blank screen into a functional, deployable repository in minutes, allowing you to focus on logic rather than boilerplate setup.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor on an empty IDE is the graveyard of a thousand student ambitions. You have the skills, you have the hunger, but you lack the 'what.' Paralysis by analysis is the silent killer of the junior developer's career, turning potential engineers into eternal tutorial-watchers.

But what if you didn't need to 'think' of an idea? What if the architecture was generated for you, and your only job was to curate and refine? By leveraging the synergy between Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code, you shift from being a passive student to an active architect, building real-world software that actually solves problems.

steps.title: Define the Domain with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a high-level project roadmap. We avoid 'to-do list' apps and focus on micro-SaaS tools that solve actual developer friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Generate a project scope for a 'Developer Productivity Tool' that helps students track their GitHub contribution trends. Include a tech stack recommendation (Next.js, Tailwind, Supabase).

steps.title: Initialize the Git Workflow
steps.description: Establish a professional version control habit immediately. We use Git not just for storage, but as a mechanism to track AI-generated iteration cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .gitignore file optimized for a Next.js and Supabase project, ensuring sensitive environment variables are strictly excluded.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'feat: initialize project structure with AI'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate the Antigravity plan into functional code. Focus on the 'happy path' first to ensure you have a working MVP within an hour.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core data schema for a user contribution tracker that fetches data from the GitHub API and stores it in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the database schema and API route to fetch GitHub user data'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement with Commits
steps.description: Never write code without a commit. This creates a history of your decision-making process, which is the most valuable asset in your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase and suggest three high-impact UI improvements for the dashboard view that would impress a hiring manager.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the UI improvements and create a new git branch for the feature'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: A project is only 'real' once it is live. We finalize the build and prepare the README to explain the 'Why' behind your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this project, highlighting the technical challenges solved and the stack used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate documentation and prepare the build pipeline for deployment'

pro_tips: Treat every AI-generated block as a pull request; review the code as if you were a senior engineer checking a junior's work.
Use Git tags to mark 'milestone versions' so you can demo different stages of your project growth during interviews.
Keep your Git commit messages descriptive—start with 'feat:', 'fix:', or 'docs:' to build a professional-grade contribution graph.
When Claude Code gets stuck, ask it to 'explain the logic' before asking it to write more code to maintain your own mental model.
Always add a 'Learnings' section to your README where you document the specific AI-induced bugs you solved; this proves deep technical understanding.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, push it to GitHub and write a LinkedIn post tagging the technologies used to gain visibility among potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student waiting for an assignment; you are a builder defining the future. Don't let your code sit on your hard drive. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
